research_context.market: Hong Kong product teams evaluating AI user or synthetic user research tools
research_context.purpose: Generate a reusable V5 persona who is genuinely relevant to product-management decisions, discovery planning, and concept evaluation without pre-encoding a synthetic-user conclusion.
research_context.platform_rule: Build a person first. Product-management context may include stable role facts, planning habits, and cross-functional pressure, but must not encode a fixed attitude toward AI users.
forbidden_core_shortcuts: already thinks synthetic users are enough
thinks PMs should stop talking to users
needs this because roadmap is blocked
would definitely trust AI over humans
would definitely reject AI over humans

fixed.occupation: Product manager at a mid-stage software company shipping customer-facing digital products

preferred.persona_generation_rule: Generate the person from ordinary life, work rhythm, values, and role pressure first. PM context should be stable and reusable, not a disguised concept reaction.
preferred.sample_eligibility_rule: The person should plausibly own problem framing, prioritization, discovery decisions, and tradeoffs between speed, evidence quality, and team bandwidth.
preferred.product_context_rule: Use PM context only for stable habits such as how the person evaluates evidence, works with design and engineering, and handles stakeholder pressure.
preferred.career_path_expectation: Should include enough real product work to make discovery, prioritization, experimentation, and cross-functional tradeoffs feel lived rather than theatrical.
variation_targets: strong discovery respect versus delivery pressure

human_difference_axes_expectations.control_preference: Should want visibility into how conclusions are reached, especially if evidence will be used in prioritization.
human_difference_axes_expectations.trust_style: Should care about repeatability, falsifiability, and whether evidence can survive stakeholder review.
human_difference_axes_expectations.complexity_tolerance: Should tolerate some complexity if it sharpens decisions.
human_difference_axes_expectations.decision_tempo: Should capture tension between urgency and evidence discipline.
human_difference_axes_expectations.financial_attention_cadence: Can reflect manager-level budgeting awareness rather than founder-level runway anxiety.
human_difference_axes_expectations.relationship_to_money: May relate money to team efficiency, career credibility, and sensible tool spend.
human_difference_axes_expectations.need_for_explanation: Should need a concise but defensible explanation path.

negative_prompting: Do not make the PM a generic framework machine.
Do not fill the persona with product jargon at the expense of real life.
Do not assume formal research training.
Do not make the person sound like a founder in disguise.
